result.question:
仓储库位分配(SLAP/slotting)的"场景→算法匹配"问题深度研究,三个子问题:

【1. 策略分类学与标准术语】仓储 storage assignment 策略的完整学术分类:经典三族(randomized / class-based / full-turnover-based storage,Hausman-Schwarz-Graves 1976 起)、规则类(Closest Open Location、COI cube-per-order index、duration-of-stay、correlated/affinity storage 等)、现代类(元启发式 GA/ALNS、精确 MIP/LAP、学习型 DRL)。每个策略给:标准英文学术名+中文惯用译名+奠基文献+一句话适用前提。特别要:我们现有五策略的正名——①随机可行位 ②固定扫描序第一可行位(sequential/first-fit?) ③行程时间最小可行位(Closest Open Location?) ④多准则加权评分在线选位 ⑤批量排序分配+局部搜索(Rank-and-Assign类)——这五个在文献里的准确对应名称。

【2. 场景→策略占优的实证知识】什么仓库/需求特征决定哪个策略占优:需求偏斜度(ABC curve shape / demand skewness)、填充率(space utilization)、批量可见性(在线逐件 vs 批量已知)、SKU 数与库位比、出入库比例。找系统性对比研究:Hausman/Graves 解析结论(class-based 接近 full-turnover 的条件)、Petersen 系列仿真对比、Gu et al. 2007 / de Koster 2007 / Reyes 2019 综述里的策略对比结论、以及"需求偏斜低时简单规则追平复杂优化"这类结论的文献出处。有定量结论(百分比)的优先。

【3. 算法选择/自适应切换的先例】Algorithm Selection Problem(Rice 1976)与 per-instance algorithm selection 在组合优化的应用(SATzilla、AutoFolio、Kerschke 2019 survey);仓储领域"根据批次画像/工况切换 slotting 策略"的论文、工业实践(dynamic slotting 产品如 Lucas/Manhattan/Blue Yonder 的场景管理逻辑)、学生竞赛或数模竞赛里做过"多工况策略切换"的方案;Müller et al. 2022 EJOR 仓储策略选择论文的具体方法与结论细化(特征、选择器形式、增益幅度)。

背景:我们是 20列×20层 AS/RS 单堆垛机,货物五属性(重量/频次/体积等),已有五策略+30seed 统计框架,要建"场景检测器+策略库"(按批次画像自动选算法,最终蒸馏成 PLC 结构化文本 if-else)。研究目的:①策略库候选补全(还缺哪些值得实现的)②检测器的特征与规则有文献依据③报告术语全部学术正名。搜索 agent 请用低档模型多派;证据要求:一手文献/官方文档优先,给 URL,区分"已核对原文/摘要级";找不到的点如实标注。

confirmed.claim: Hausman, Schwarz & Graves (1976, Management Science 22(6):629-638) is the foundational paper establishing the classic three-way taxonomy of AS/RS storage assignment policies: random assignment, full turnover-based assignment, and class-based turnover assignment — confirming the terminology attribution in the research question's strategy taxonomy.
confirmed.quote: Results are obtained which compare the operating performance of three storage assignment rules: random assignment, which is similar to the closest-open-location rule used by many currently operating systems; full turnover-based assignment: and class-based turnover assignment.

confirmed.claim: The paper's headline analytical result is that turnover-based storage rules (full and class-based) achieve significant reductions in stacker crane travel time and distance relative to random assignment. Note: the abstract asserts this only qualitatively — the specific quantitative percentages (e.g., how closely class-based with few classes approaches full-turnover performance under different ABC skewness) are in the paywalled full text and could not be verified from this page (abstract-level evidence only).
confirmed.quote: It is shown that significant reductions in crane travel time (and distance) are obtainable from turnover-based rules.

confirmed.claim: Per the review, Hausman et al. (1976) argue that closest open location storage and random storage perform similarly under full-pallet (unit-load) movement, and closest-open-location characteristically fills racks near the depot first, emptying toward the back — a documented condition under which a simple greedy rule ties random assignment.
confirmed.quote: Hausman et al. (1976) argue that closest open location storage and random storage have a similar performance if products are moved by full pallets only.

confirmed.claim: The cube-per-order index (COI) rule is attributed to Heskett (1963, 1964) as an early full-turnover policy: COI = ratio of an item's total required space to the number of trips required to satisfy its demand per period, with lowest-COI items located closest to the depot; the review notes COI-based assignment requires an information-intensive approach (Caron et al. 1998) and frequent demand/assortment changes cause costly reshuffling.
confirmed.quote: An early storage policy of this type is the cube-per-order index (COI) rule, see Heskett (1963, 1964). The COI of an item is defined as the ratio of the item's total required space to the number of trips required to satisfy its demand per period.

confirmed.claim: Goetschalckx & Ratliff (1990), Management Science 36(9):1120-1132, is the foundational paper that defines and names duration-of-stay (DOS) based shared storage policies for unit-load warehouses — the correct 奠基文献 for the 'duration-of-stay storage' entry in the SLAP strategy taxonomy (distinct from turnover/COI-based policies).
confirmed.quote: This paper addresses duration-of-stay-based shared storage policies for a unit load warehouse.

confirmed.claim: The COI rule was originally proposed by J. L. Heskett (before this 1976 paper; the abstract says only 'several years ago' and does not itself give the 1963 date) and at origin was justified only heuristically via numerical examples, not by optimization theory — so founding-literature attribution for COI should be Heskett (proposal) plus Kallina & Lynn 1976 (optimality proof/application).
confirmed.quote: Several years ago, J. L. Heskett proposed a criterion, called the cube-per-order index (CPO) rule, for solving this problem. The criterion was justified heuristically by means of numerical examples.

confirmed.claim: Reyes et al. (2019) attribute the three classic storage-policy families — random storage, dedicated storage, class-based storage — to Hausman et al. (1976), and attribute two additional rule policies (closest-free-space and inventory-rotation/turnover) to Gómez et al. (2008); the paper defines closest-free-space as prioritizing available locations at shorter distance from reception/dispatch areas, i.e. the policy family matching the user's strategy ③ (Closest Open Location).
confirmed.quote: Hausman et al. (1976) observe that there are various storage space allocation policies, among which the most representative are random storage, dedicated storage, and class-based storage. In addition, Gómez et al. (2008) introduced storage policies that depend on the closest free space and inventory rotation. In short, the random policy uses any location for storage products, while the closest free space policy prioritizes storage locations available at shorter distances from the reception or dispatching areas.

confirmed.claim: The review's catalog of storage policies used in the 2005–2017 SLAP literature fixes the standard English names needed for terminology correction: class-based (CB), cube order per index (COI) [note: this paper's word order; usually 'cube-per-order index'], correlation-based assignment (CoB), full turnover-based storage (TOB), random-based (RB), shared-based (SB), dedicated storage (DSP), closest-open location (COL), duration-of-stay based (DSB), and frequency-based (FB), plus rules (horizontal/vertical assignment, fuzzy rules) and newer proposals (rearrange-while-working, autonomous cell based, energy consumption based).
confirmed.quote: The policies used in the analyzed publications were: class-based (CB), cube order per index (COI), correlation-based assignment (CoB), full turnover-based storage (TOB), random-based (RB), shared-based (SB), dedicated storage (DSP), closest-open location (COL), duration-of-stay based (DSB), and frequency-based (FB). In addition, rules such as horizontal assignment (HA), vertical assignment (VA), lower and upper assignment (ULA) or fuzzy rules (FR) have also been used. Newer proposals are also appearing, such as rearrange-while-working (RWW), autonomous cell based (ACB), and energy consumption based (EB).

confirmed.claim: The paper finds SLAP terminology is not standardized: 'storage assignment' and 'storage location assignment' together account for 81% of term usage in the reviewed literature (other labels: product allocation/location 7%, storage location/space 5%, reserve/space allocation 6%, slotting 1%), and the authors explicitly recommend standardizing on 'storage location assignment problem (SLAP)'.
confirmed.quote: We found that the most-used terms were storage assignment and storage location assignment, which together make up 81% of the examples in the present literature review... We found that there is no standard terminology for the SLAP, as many authors refer to it with varying labels, such as product allocation, product location, reserve allocation, space allocation, or slotting. Therefore, we would suggest that the term should be standardized to storage location assignment problem (SLAP).

confirmed.claim: 【已核对原文摘要】Gu, Goetschalckx & McGinnis (2007) 的综述按四大基本仓库功能(receiving 收货、storage 储存、order picking 拣选、shipping 发运)对全部仓库运作规划问题进行分类,storage(含库位分配 SLAP)是其中一个一级类别——该四功能框架是 SLAP 策略分类学的标准综述级锚点,可用于报告术语正名。
confirmed.quote: An extensive review on warehouse operation planning problems is presented. The problems are classified according to the basic warehouse functions, i.e., receiving, storage, order picking, and shipping.

confirmed.claim: The review enumerates a canonical list of named storage policies used in the 2005-2017 SLAP literature: class-based (CB), cube-per-order index (COI), correlation-based assignment (CoB), full turnover-based storage (TOB), random-based (RB), shared-based (SB), dedicated storage (DSP), closest-open location (COL), duration-of-stay based (DSB), and frequency-based (FB), plus assignment rules (horizontal/vertical/upper-lower assignment, fuzzy rules) and newer proposals (rearrange-while-working, autonomous-cell-based, energy-consumption-based). This directly supplies the standard English names for a strategy library: the user's strategy ① maps to random-based storage, ③ to closest-open location (COL), and duration-of-stay (DSB) appears as a distinct named policy (used by Meneghetti & Monti 2013/2014 and Chen et al. 2010 per Table 7). [已核对原文全文]
confirmed.quote: The policies used in the analyzed publications were: class-based (CB), cube order per index (COI), correlation-based assignment (CoB), full turnover-based storage (TOB), random-based (RB), shared-based (SB), dedicated storage (DSP), closest-open location (COL), duration-of-stay based (DSB), and frequency-based (FB). In addition, rules such as horizontal assignment (HA), vertical assignment (VA), lower and upper assignment (ULA) or fuzzy rules (FR) have also been used. Newer proposals are also appearing, such as rearrange-while-working (RWW), autonomous cell based (ACB), and energy consumption based (EB).

confirmed.claim: Medrano-Zarazúa, Saucedo-Martínez & Bolaños-Zuñiga (COMPSE 2022 proceedings, Springer, first online 2023-05-23, pp. 15-37) is a systematic literature review of SLAP papers published 2005–2021 that classifies studies by solution methodology, objectives, and parameters to identify the most-used solution methods — making it a recent taxonomy-bearing review complementary to Gu 2007/de Koster 2007/Reyes 2019, though its detailed classification tables are paywalled (only abstract + references verified).
confirmed.quote: through a systematic literature review, different academic papers published between 2005 and 2021 on the SLAP are analyzed and classified according to the solution methodology, objectives, and other characteristics, so that the most used methods in the literature are obtained for this type of problem as well as the parameters that are most taken into account to solve it

refuted.claim: HSG 1976 explicitly treats random assignment as operationally similar to the closest-open-location (COL) rule used by then-operating systems — i.e., in the founding paper COL is modeled as equivalent to randomized storage, not as a distinct optimization policy. This bears directly on the correct academic naming of the user's strategy ① (random feasible location) vs ③ (travel-time-minimizing feasible location, which is literal COL): the two are conflated in this source under high space utilization.

The review establishes a five-type taxonomy of frequently used storage assignment policies — random storage, closest open location storage, dedicated storage, full-turnover storage, and class-based storage — treating family grouping (correlated storage, in complimentary-based and contact-based variants) as a separate relations-aware complement. This directly supplies the standard academic names for the user's strategies: ① = random storage (Petersen 1997 cited), ③ ≈ closest open location storage.

On class-based vs full-turnover storage: Petersen et al. (2004) simulation shows full-turnover storage outperforms class-based storage on travel distance in manual order picking, but 2-4 classes are recommended in practice for ease of implementation and administration; for AS/RS specifically, Yang (1988) and Van den Berg and Gademann (2000) found 6 classes best in their studies. The review adds that class-based storage requires more rack space than randomized storage (Graves et al. 1977) because empty slots must be available per class region.

For AS/RS class-based storage, Hausman et al. (1976) proved L-shaped, square-in-time class regions optimal for mean single-command travel time (with optimal two-class boundaries derived analytically; extended to n classes by Rosenblatt and Eynan 1989); for dual-command cycles Graves et al. (1977) showed by simulation such L-shaped regions are not necessarily optimal but stay within 3% of the optimum — a quantitative bound relevant to a 20x20 single-crane AS/RS.

The paper's stated applicability premise: the DOS policy proven optimal with respect to both travel time and storage space holds specifically for systems with balanced input and output (balanced inbound/outbound flows); for more complex (unbalanced) systems only heuristic DOS policies are offered.

Kallina & Lynn (1976) report that the cube-per-order index (COI, abbreviated 'CPO' in the original) rule is not merely a heuristic: the stock-location problem class posed by Heskett can be formulated as a linear program, and the COI rule is exactly the optimal solution of that LP (the proof is attributed to prior work by one of the authors, i.e., Kallina, and summarized in this paper). This is the standard citation establishing COI optimality for dedicated storage under linear travel-cost assumptions.

【摘要级/二手确认,未核对付费全文】该综述将 SLAP 按信息可得性分层分类:item information(单件信息完全已知→对应 dedicated 类)、product information(仅产品级聚合信息→对应 class-based/duration-of-stay 类)、no information(无信息→对应 random/closest-open-location 类);其中 SLAP/Product-Information 情形定义为:件与件可互换、单件到达/离开时刻不再预先可知、仅有历史订单或聚合统计。这直接对应研究问题中'批量可见性(在线逐件 vs 批量已知)决定策略族'的文献依据。

The review attributes the classic policy-family classification to Hausman et al. (1976) — random storage, dedicated storage, and class-based storage as 'most representative' — and defines the closest-free-space policy (locations at shorter distances from reception/dispatch prioritized, attributed to Gómez et al. 2008) and inventory-rotation policy (higher-sales items stored closest to reception/dispatch) as additional families. This confirms Hausman et al. 1976 as the foundational reference for the classic three families and gives a citable definition matching the user's strategy ③ (closest open location). [已核对原文全文]

The review explicitly identifies the design of dynamic storage policies (especially in automated systems, citing Chen et al. 2010), performance testing of different solution types depending on each warehouse's configuration (citing Boysen & Stephan 2013), and machine-learning/AI-based decision-support tools as open future-research directions as of 2018 — i.e., 'scenario-dependent policy selection' was flagged as a gap, not solved territory, in this literature through 2017, which supports the novelty framing of a scenario-detector + strategy-library approach. [已核对原文全文]

该综述给出的策略分类学(其自身贡献,Figure 1):随机(文中称 haphazard)与 class-based 同属 shared storage(共享库位);haphazard 族的标准成员为 random assignment、closest open location、farthest open location、longest open location(引 Gu et al 2007)——即我们的策略①对应 random assignment、策略③候选名 Closest Open Location 确为文献标准术语;而 turnover-based storage 与 full-turnover、volume-based 为同义词,在文献中通常代表 dedicated policy。[已核对原文全文]

综述转述 Hausman, Schwarz & Graves (1976, Management Science 22(6):629-638) 的解析结论:当货物以整托盘(full pallets / unit-load)出入时,Closest Open Location 与 random storage 性能相同——对我们的启示:单元货载 AS/RS 场景下策略①(随机可行位)与就近选位在长期性能上可能不可区分,策略③若按行程时间最小选位应与 COL 明确区分命名。[综述级转述,Hausman 原文未直接核对]

定量结论(综述转述 Petersen & Aase 2004, IJPE 92(1):11-19):turnover-based dedicated 策略优于三分类 class-based 策略,但改进幅度不足 1%;且 Muppani & Adil (2008a) 指出因 class 内随机存放降低所需存储面积、缩短拣选行程,这一微弱优势甚至可能不成立——即'需求分类粗糙的简单策略追平完全排序优化'有具体百分比出处。[综述级转述]

unverified.claim: 场景→策略占优条件(综述转述):Guo & de Koster (2015/2016, IJPR 54(8)) 证明 turnover-based(full-turnover)的平均单程行驶距离并非仓库的下界,原因是空间共享——shared/haphazard 存储所需空间仅约为 turnover-based 的三分之二(almost two third),空间需求差异反过来影响平均行程;且 Muppani & Adil (2008b, Omega 36(4)) 观察到 SKU 库存水平波动大的系统中 class-based 优于 dedicated——即填充率/空间利用与库存波动性是决定共享类策略反超专用类策略的两个文献依据特征。[综述级转述]
